product_type: Non-Linked Participating Savings Life Insurance Plan
summary: A participating savings life insurance plan offering guaranteed maturity benefits, death benefits, and survival benefits through cash bonuses and paid-up additions. Available with three plan options: Life Goal Maximiser, Bright Future Maximiser, and Golden Years Maximiser. Includes optional spouse cover and various rider benefits.

definitions.definition: The guaranteed maturity benefit payable on the death of Life Insured/Primary Life Insured or maturity of the Policy
Survival benefit payable by the Company calculated as Cash Bonus Rate multiplied by Basic Sum Assured under the Policy. Not guaranteed and applicable if all due premiums are paid
Additional guaranteed benefits payable on death or maturity, purchased using declared cash bonuses
30 days from due date for annual, half-yearly and quarterly mode; 15 days for monthly mode for premium payment without penalty
Highest of: 11 times Annualized Premium, Basic Sum Assured, or 105% of all Premiums paid till date of death

important_conditions.condition: Minimum age at entry is 90 days for Life Insured, 18 years for Primary Life Insured and Spouse
Maximum entry age varies by Premium Payment Term: 6 years (50 years), 8/10/12 years (55 years), 15 years (50 years)
Maximum age for Spouse at entry is 60 years
Maturity age is fixed at 85 years
Policy lapses if premiums for first two consecutive years are not paid within Grace Period
Surrender value acquired after payment of 2 consecutive full years' premiums
Revival allowed within 5 years from first unpaid premium date

faqs.question: What are the three plan options available?
faqs.answer: Life Goal Maximiser, Bright Future Maximiser, and Golden Years Maximiser. The plan option chosen at inception cannot be changed during the Policy Term.

faqs.question: What is the minimum and maximum entry age?
faqs.answer: Minimum age is 90 days for Life Insured, 18 years for Primary Life Insured and Spouse. Maximum entry age depends on Premium Payment Term and ranges from 50-55 years for Primary Life Insured, 60 years for Spouse.

faqs.question: What is the Grace Period for premium payment?
faqs.answer: 30 days from due date for annual, half-yearly and quarterly mode; 15 days for monthly mode, without levy of interest or penalty.

faqs.question: How is Sum Assured on Death calculated?
faqs.answer: Highest of: 11 times Annualized Premium plus extra premium, Basic Sum Assured, or 105% of all Premiums paid till date of death.

faqs.question: When does the policy lapse?
faqs.answer: If premiums for the first two consecutive Policy years are not paid within the Grace Period, the policy lapses from the due date of the first unpaid premium.

faqs.question: When can I surrender the policy?
faqs.answer: Surrender value is acquired after payment of 2 consecutive full years' premiums. You can surrender anytime after that before maturity.

faqs.question: What is the revival period?
faqs.answer: You can revive a lapsed policy within 5 years from the date of first unpaid premium and before maturity date.

faqs.question: What are the different bonus options available?
faqs.answer: Cash Bonus Payout - Immediate, Cash Bonus Payout - Deferred, and Paid-Up Additions option.

faqs.question: How does the Cash Bonus Payout - Immediate option work?
faqs.answer: Cash bonuses are payable at the end of each policy year. First year bonus is payable at end of 13th policy month. You can choose yearly or monthly payout.

faqs.question: What is the Cash Bonus Payout - Deferred option?
faqs.answer: You can defer cash bonus for 5, 7, or 10 years. No cash bonus is declared during the deferment period. Payouts start after the deferment period ends.

faqs.question: How do Paid-Up Additions work?
faqs.answer: Cash bonuses are used to purchase additional sum assured (Paid-Up Additions) using Paid-Up Addition factors. These additions also participate in future bonuses.

faqs.question: Can I change my bonus option during the policy term?
faqs.answer: You can change from Cash Payout to Paid-Up Addition once during policy term by giving written request 3 months prior to policy anniversary. Change from Paid-Up Addition to Cash Payout is not allowed.

faqs.question: What is the Spouse Cover Option?
faqs.answer: An option covering two lives - Primary Life Insured and Spouse. Spouse sum assured can range from 50% to 100% of Primary Life Insured's Basic Sum Assured.

faqs.question: What happens on death of Primary Life Insured under Spouse Cover?
faqs.answer: Death benefit is paid, future premiums are waived, and spouse cover continues until spouse reaches 85 years or policy term ends.

faqs.question: What riders are available?
faqs.answer: Kotak Accidental Death Benefit, Permanent Disability Benefit, Term Benefit, Life Guardian Benefit, Accidental Disability Guardian Benefit, and Critical Illness Plus Benefit riders.

faqs.question: What are the modal factors for premium payment?
faqs.answer: Yearly: 100%, Half-yearly: 51%, Quarterly: 26%, Monthly: 8.8% of yearly premium.

faqs.question: What is Minor Revival?
faqs.answer: Revival within 6 months from first unpaid premium due date without evidence of good health, paying outstanding premiums plus 9% simple interest.

faqs.question: What is Major Revival?
faqs.answer: Revival after 6 months but within 5 years, requiring evidence of good health and payment of premiums in arrears plus 9% simple interest.

faqs.question: How is Guaranteed Surrender Value calculated?
faqs.answer: Percentage of total premiums paid (per GSV factors table) less total applicable survival benefits paid, or zero, whichever is higher.

faqs.question: What happens when policy becomes Reduced Paid-Up?
faqs.answer: Basic Sum Assured is reduced proportionately based on premiums paid vs. total premiums payable. No future bonuses are declared unless policy is revived.

faqs.question: Are loans available under this policy?
faqs.answer: No, there are no loans provided under this policy.

faqs.question: What happens when a minor Life Insured attains majority?
faqs.answer: The policy automatically vests in the Life Insured at age 18, and the company will correspond directly with them thereafter.

faqs.question: What is the Free Look Period?
faqs.answer: 15 days for regular policies (30 days for electronic policies) to return the policy if not agreeable to provisions, stating reasons thereof.

faqs.question: How does the Bright Future Maximiser plan work?
faqs.answer: Designed for child's future, cash bonuses purchase Paid-Up Additions for selected tenure (5-18 years). At tenure end, accrued additions are paid as survival benefit.

faqs.question: How does the Golden Years Maximiser plan work?
faqs.answer: Cash bonuses purchase Paid-Up Additions until Primary Life Insured's 60th birthday, then accrued additions are paid as lump-sum survival benefit.

faqs.question: Can Paid-Up Additions be encashed during policy term?
faqs.answer: Yes, from second policy year onwards, up to 6 times per year, subject to minimum cash value of Rs. 3,000. Remaining additions continue to participate in profits.

faqs.question: What is Terminal Bonus?
faqs.answer: Additional bonus that may be declared on maturity, death (if policy completed 25 years), or surrender (if policy completed 3 years), expressed as percentage of Basic Sum Assured and Paid-Up Additions.

faqs.question: What is Interim Bonus?
faqs.answer: Bonus that may be declared for in-force premium paying policies exiting between policy anniversaries due to death or surrender, calculated proportionately from previous anniversary.

faqs.question: How is monthly cash bonus calculated?
faqs.answer: Monthly cash bonus payout = 96% × (yearly cash bonus payout) ÷ 12.
